rows.notes: Top-centre page title; very clear handwriting and no monetary amount in the title line. Vertical ruling lines not needed for this header.

Description legible but some letters ambiguous (reads as 'Southamdrington' / 'Southam...'). Amount at far right is faint and small; interpreted as '2 - 5 - 2' by alignment with the three right-hand ruling lines and comparison with clearer totals below. Because the description and small digits are both somewhat faint, confidence reduced.
Short description (dash then 'ad fest Michl') clearly visible. Amount '10 - 0 - 0' is well aligned under the pounds column and digits are crisp; shillings/pence zeroes are clear.

A second identical line (dash then 'ad fest Michl'). Amount reads '10 - 0 - 0' similar to the prior row. Slight ink fade compared with previous line but column alignment is clear so reading is reliable.
Description 'Plate Excise' is clear. Amount '5 - 15 - 0' is crisply written and aligns perfectly with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling columns.
Clear description and amount '0 - 5 - 0' well aligned. No ambiguity in digits.

rows.description: Pauperibus (general Collection for ye Poor Jersey 1769.)
rows.notes: Description mostly legible though 'Pauperibus' / 'general collection' require pale reading; amount '3 - 3 - 0' is clear and aligned. Year '1769' visible in parentheses.
Centered section header introducing the next group. Handwriting clear though Latin abbreviation 'Dno' (Domino) and 'Scholariis' are old-style; no amounts present.
Description reads as 'Apendium' (spelling variable in manuscript). Amount '18 - 6 - 8' is legible; column alignment clear. Some letters in description slightly faded, hence confidence < 1.0.
Description 'pro augment. South New: & Merton' reasonably clear (some abbreviations). Amount '24 - 0 - 0' clear and aligned with pounds column.

Description is abbreviated and partly uncertain ('Dno flicus' reconstructed from manuscript). Amount '33 - 6 - 8' is readable but ink slightly blotchy on the shillings/pence; column boundaries were used to confirm each digit.
Description 'Divid: Dna flicus' (abbreviated) visible. Amount '50 - 0 - 0' is written clearly with strong strokes, alignment consistent with other rows.
Single-word description 'Stipend' is clear. Amount '31 - 0 - 0' clearly written and validated against ruling lines.
Bracketed description '(pro 3a parte Kidling: augment.)' reasonably legible though some letters ambiguous. Amount '22 - 0 - 10' is clear; pence '10' validated as <=11.
Another 'Stipend' line; amount '19 - 0 - 0' clear and well aligned.
Bracketed description for a 4th part augment; letters slightly cramped but readable. Amount '11 - 5 - 5' is legible; all currency components pass validation.

Description similar to earlier 'Stipend Dno flicus' but ink heavier here; amount reads '35 - 6 - 8'. Slight variability in handwriting makes the first digit (3) and subsequent digits clear but description abbreviation uncertain.
Second occurrence of 'Divid: Dna flicus'. Amount '50 - 0 - 0' clear and matches earlier similar line; alignment strong.

Bracketed description '(Pro augment: Longwith.)' legible. Amount reads '27 - 19 - 0' with a small handwritten fractional mark after the pence that matches a three‑farthings symbol (looks like '3q' or a handwritten '¾'); interpreted as 0.75 (three farthings). The fraction is faint but consistent with the final page total fraction.
Bracketed 'Pro augment Gwinear' (spelling possibly 'Gwinear') readable. Amount '24 - 10 - 0' clear and aligns well with ruling columns.

Final total marked with a stylised 'L:' (pound sign). Amount reads '375 - 19 - 0' with the same three‑farthings (¾) fractional mark after the pence as seen above; alignment of the total with the three ruling lines is good and confirms earlier row fractions. All components validated (shillings <20, pence whole <=11).
